data.title: Small caps outshine tech elite in 2026; Russell 2000 hits record highs, up 7% YTD. Small caps beat S&P for 10 days.
data.description: In 2026, the Russell 2000 index started the year strong by reaching new highs, overshadowing the tech giants on Wall Street. The small-cap benchmark, monitored through the iShares Russell 2000 ETF (NYSE: IWM), has achieved record highs during the last 10 trading sessions as of Jan. 15. Throughout this period, small-cap stocks have outperformed large caps consistently, surpassing the benchmarks of the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY). This unbroken streak is reminiscent of June 2008. Year-to-date, the Russell 2000 has surged over 7%, significantly surpassing the S&P 500's meager 1.5% growth. On the contrary, the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (NYSE: MAGS) has seen no growth, remaining flat for the year. Small caps have stolen the spotlight in early 2026 by outperforming the S&P 500 for the longest stretch since 2008. The driving force behind this rally has been the improving macroeconomic conditions, leading to a more positive earnings outlook for smaller companies.

data.title: Rapid7's Q4 and FY 2025 financial results will be reported on February 10.
data.description: A new report reveals that the global economy is projected to grow by 4.4% in 2022, up from a growth rate of 4.1% in 2021. This increase is largely due to the ongoing recovery from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. Advanced economies are expected to grow by 3.9% in 2022, while emerging and developing economies are forecasted to grow by 4.7%. Inflation is also expected to remain a concern, with global inflation projected to reach 3.9% in 2022. Unemployment rates are expected to decrease slightly, with the global unemployment rate predicted to drop to 5.5% in 2022 from 5.7% in 2021. Overall, the outlook for the global economy in 2022 is positive, although challenges such as inflation and geopolitical tensions remain.

data.title: Jurek Kozyra of Nanovery introduces DNA nanorobots on podcast, reducing diagnostic wait times, revolutionizing medicine.
data.description: CEO Jurek Kozyra from Nanovery recently appeared on the Disruption Interruption podcast with host Karla Jo Helms to talk about transforming molecular medicine. During the conversation, Kozyra shared groundbreaking advancements using DNA nanorobots and AI that have slashed diagnostic wait times from days to mere hours. These innovations are also expediting drug development processes, showing promise in significantly impacting the industry.

data.title: NextVolt sells 250 MW / 1,000 MWh battery storage project to Exus Renewables North America.
data.description: NextVolt, a prominent utility-scale battery energy storage developer based in Reston, Virginia, revealed on January 15, 2026, that it has finalized the sale of its standalone battery energy storage project. The project boasts an impressive capacity of 250 megawatts (MW) and a substantial energy storage capacity of 1,000 megawatt-hours (MWh). The buyer, Exus Renewables North America, a well-known owner-operator in the industry, has acquired this significant project from NextVolt.
